4 Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_SI_ARCTIC_transport 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-ARC-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-SI 
Issue 3.2 – March 2026 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_CLIMATE_SI_ARCTIC_transport 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
 
The time series provided here represents: 
• Southward integrated sea -ice area and volume transport through the entire 
Fram Strait between Spitsbergen and Greenland 
• Southward integrated sea -ice area and volume transport into the northern 
Barents Sea between Svalbard and Franz Josef Land  
• Westward integrated sea-ice area and volume transport into the eastern Barents 
Sea between Novaya Zemlya and Franz Josef Land 
 
The sea-ice area export (km 2 month-1) is calculated as the sum of the width of each 
model grid cell times the sea-ice concentration within each grid cell times the sea-ice 
velocity perpendicular to the section. The sea -ice volume transport (km 3 month-1) is 
calculated similarly, but also includes each grid cell’s average sea -ice thickness. The 
time series of modelled sea -ice area and volume transport are evaluated against 
observation-based estimates reported in literature, assessing both the long -term 
averages and trends, and the seasonal variations.
